title: Secure Cookie Configuration (Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite)
description:
**CRITICAL SECURITY REQUIREMENT**

All session cookies must be configured with proper security flags to prevent session hijacking and cross-site attacks.

## Required Cookie Flags

- `Secure` — only sent over HTTPS
- `HttpOnly` — not accessible via JavaScript
- `SameSite=Lax` — CSRF protection (compatible with OAuth/SAML redirects)
- `Path=/`
- `Max-Age` matching session policy

## Cookie Domain Decision

- **Option A**: `.datatoolspro.app` (shared across all subdomains) — simpler, less isolation
- **Option B**: Host-specific (e.g., `toyota.us1.datatoolspro.app`) — better isolation, more complex

## Cookie Template

```
Set-Cookie: session_jwt=<value>; Secure; HttpOnly; SameSite=Lax; Path=/; Domain=<chosen>; Max-Age=<policy>
```

**Security impact: CRITICAL** — missing Secure flag enables session hijacking over HTTP.

**Applies to**: MBT-1274 (code exchange sets cookie), MBT-1286 (logout clears cookie)
acceptance_criteria: All session cookies include Secure flag
All session cookies include HttpOnly flag
Cookie domain strategy is documented and implemented
Cookie max-age matches the session lifetime policy
Cookie configuration is consistent across code exchange and logout endpoints
middleware_changes: Centralized cookie configuration ensuring all session cookies are set with Secure, HttpOnly, and SameSite=Lax flags. Applied consistently in code exchange and logout handlers.
implementation_notes: Cookie domain decision impacts the entire auth architecture. Shared parent domain (.datatoolspro.app) is simpler but provides less tenant isolation. Host-specific cookies provide better isolation but require per-tenant cookie management. SameSite=Lax is chosen over Strict because Strict breaks OAuth/SAML redirect flows.
